meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building high-end SaaS projects fast. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver professional results for clients.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a bottleneck, not a dead end. By leveraging a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you can bypass manual boilerplate and ship production-grade features in hours. Use Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to iterate features, transforming your output from zero to high-value SaaS assets immediately.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the silence of a non-existent portfolio echoing louder than your ambition. Clients aren't looking for promises; they are looking for proof of competence. Every hour you spend reinventing the wheel is an hour you aren't closing a deal or shipping a product that commands a premium fee.

It’s time to stop building from scratch and start building with leverage. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for surgical feature implementation, you can transform from a 'developer with no work' into a 'SaaS architect with a deployment track record.' This is how you reclaim your time and build the professional reputation you deserve.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Blueprint
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core value prop. We use Antigravity to generate a scalable project architecture so you don't waste time on folder structures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready SaaS starter kit using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include authentication, Stripe integration, and a clean responsive dashboard layout.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema
steps.description: Database design is where most freelancers slow down. Use Claude Code to translate your business logic into a robust SQL schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform including tables for users, subscriptions, and project entities with Row Level Security enabled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate sql schema with rls for user and subscription tables"

steps.title: Implement Feature Modules
steps.description: Break the app into micro-features. This modular approach allows you to showcase specific skills like API handling or state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a reusable data-table component that fetches data from a Supabase client and supports server-side pagination.

steps.title: Integrate Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Nothing proves professional capability like a working Stripe checkout flow. Use Claude to handle the webhook complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks for subscription management and sync status with the local database user table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "setup stripe webhook handler for subscription.updated event"

steps.title: Polishing for Production
steps.description: Finalize your portfolio piece by adding error boundaries and professional loading states. This separates amateur code from client-ready software.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add error handling and loading skeletons to all asynchronous data fetching components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "audit code for loading states and error boundaries"

pro_tips: Always include a 'README.md' that explains your architectural decisions; clients care more about your thought process than the code itself.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your core auth flow; it shows you prioritize security, a major selling point for high-paying clients.
Focus on one 'hero' feature in your starter kit that solves a specific industry problem, rather than building a generic 'to-do' app clone.
Keep your git history clean; use descriptive commit messages so your portfolio repository looks like a professional team project.
Always deploy to Vercel or Railway immediately; a live link is worth ten times more than a GitHub repo.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a custom domain, add a 'Case Study' section to your personal website linking to this repo, and start pitching to clients using this starter kit as your primary demo.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to find you. Build the proof they need today and watch your lead flow transform. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
